title: Mutton Karahi

seo.title: Mutton Karahi Recipe: Authentic Pakistani Dinner Classic
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Mutton Karahi with our authentic Pakistani recipe. Learn to make this flavorful, spicy dinner dish step-by-step at home. Perfect for any occasion!
lowCompetitionKeywords: Mutton Karahi with tomatoes and ginger
seo.h1: Authentic Mutton Karahi Recipe: A Pakistani Classic
h2Headings: Step-by-Step Guide to Making Mutton Karahi
Pro Tips for the Best Mutton Karahi
seo.imageAlt: A vibrant plate of authentic Mutton Karahi, a traditional Pakistani dinner recipe, garnished with fresh ginger and green chilies.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, spicy flavors of homemade Mutton Karahi, a beloved Pakistani dinner dish.

description: Mutton Karahi is a quintessential Pakistani dish, celebrated for its rich, spicy, and tangy flavors. This robust curry features tender mutton cooked in a wok (karahi) with tomatoes, ginger, garlic, and a blend of aromatic spices, creating a deeply satisfying meal. It's a beloved staple, perfect for family dinners or festive gatherings, best enjoyed with hot naan or roti.

instructions.title: Sear the Mutton
instructions.text: Heat oil or ghee in a large karahi or wok over high heat. Add the mutton pieces and sear until they change color and are lightly browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes. Remove the mutton and set aside, leaving the oil in the karahi.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics and Tomatoes
instructions.text: In the same oil, add chopped onions (if using) and sauté until translucent. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant. Then add the chopped tomatoes and cook until they soften and release their juices, forming a thick paste, about 8-10 minutes. Mash the tomatoes with the back of a spoon as they cook.

instructions.title: Add Spices and Mutton
instructions.text: Stir in red chili powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, and salt. Cook the spices for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, adding a splash of water if needed to prevent burning. Return the seared mutton to the karahi and mix well to coat with the spice mixture.

instructions.title: Simmer to Tenderize
instructions.text: Add 1 cup of water, cover the karahi, and reduce heat to low. Let the mutton simmer until it is tender. This can take anywhere from 20-30 minutes, depending on the cut of mutton. Check occasionally and add more water if the gravy dries out.

instructions.title: Finish with Yogurt and Green Chilies
instructions.text: Once the mutton is tender and the gravy has thickened, increase the heat to medium-high. Add the whipped yogurt and green chilies. Cook, stirring continuously, until the oil separates from the gravy and the yogurt is fully incorporated and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. This step is crucial for the karahi's texture and flavor.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Stir in the garam masala powder. Garnish generously with julienned fresh ginger and chopped fresh coriander leaves. Serve hot with fresh naan, roti, or plain rice.

tips: For truly authentic flavor, ensure your karahi (wok) is piping hot before adding the mutton. High heat searing helps lock in juices and develop a rich crust.
The 'bhunai' (stir-frying) of the masala until oil separates is key to a rich, well-developed karahi. Don't rush this step, as it builds the depth of flavor.
Always add yogurt on medium heat and stir continuously to prevent it from curdling. Freshly crushed ginger and green chilies at the end elevate the aroma and spice.
article: Mutton Karahi is a beloved Pakistani Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
